steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage introduces the energy ladder model as a widely accepted explanation, then uses 'however' and 'reductive' to signal disagreement. The main purpose is to push back on that model.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To challenge a common explanation for households' fuel choices.’: directly names the passage's purpose — contesting the energy ladder model. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘To contrast patterns in household fuel use in two countries.’: the passage is not organized around a country-to-country comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To account for the effect of fuel use on household income.’: mischaracterizes the argument; the passage questions the model's simplicity, not its causal direction. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To critique a frequently used model of changes in household fuel prices.’: the passage critiques choice models, not a model about prices specifically. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
